name: aidrift
description: Watches your AI coding chats (Claude Code, Codex) and warns when sessions drift.

commands.title: Drift: Sign In with Email + Password
Drift: Create Checkpoint for Active Session
Drift: Show Status for Active Session
Drift: Open Active Session in Dashboard

aidrift.apiBaseUrl.description: AI Drift API base URL. Single public origin for all clients (dashboard, extension, CLI). In local dev this is the frontend container (which proxies /api/* to the api container). In SaaS deployment set this to e.g. https://aidrift.example.com/api.

aidrift.dashboardUrl.description: Dashboard URL opened by 'Drift: Open Active Session in Dashboard'.

aidrift.watchClaudeCode.description: Watch ~/.claude/projects/ for new Claude Code transcripts and auto-track them.

aidrift.watchCodex.description: Watch ~/.codex/sessions/ for new OpenAI Codex rollouts and auto-track them.

aidrift.trackTaskExecution.description: Report VSCode task and terminal shell outcomes (lint/build/test pass/fail) for the active turn so the Code Quality dashboard has data. Terminal coverage relies on VSCode shell integration (1.93+).

aidrift.watchGitEvents.description: Watch .git/refs/ for commits and pushes. Displays git events in the session timeline and analytics dashboards.

aidrift.statusPollIntervalSeconds.description: How often the status bar polls /sessions/:id/status. On repeated failures the interval grows exponentially (capped at 5 minutes) and resets on first success.

aidrift.requestTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout for HTTP requests from the extension to the AI Drift API. Requests that exceed this are aborted so a hung network doesn't wedge polling. Minimum 2 seconds.

aidrift.driftAlertThreshold.description: Score below which a drift alert fires.

aidrift.autoCheckpoint.description: Auto-create a checkpoint on every accepted turn.

aidrift.implicitAcceptTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds without a follow-up before a turn is treated as implicitly accepted.
